summary: DeVault argues that FOSS projects should stop adopting Slack as their communication platform and use IRC instead. He contends that Slack is closed source, a walled garden, and was never designed for open source communities — a point Slack itself has acknowledged. He systematically addresses the perceived advantages of Slack over IRC, arguing that IRC's solutions for persistent sessions, integrations, and code sharing are adequate or superior. The post emphasizes that IRC's open, standardized, distributed nature aligns with free software values, while Slack fragments communities across isolated tabs and proprietary infrastructure.
key_points: Slack is a closed-source walled garden that requires separate logins per project, fragmenting the open source community experience
Slack itself has admitted it cannot support the open community use case that FOSS projects need
IRC's perceived shortcomings (persistence, file sharing, integrations) all have workable solutions, and IRC is actually superior for bot integrations
IRC's longevity and massive active user base on networks like Freenode demonstrate it is far from dead
Using a single IRC client across hundreds of projects creates a network effect that Slack's siloed model destroys
money_quotes: I'd really appreciate it if we all quit using Slack like this. It's not appropriate for FOSS projects.
I put a lot more faith in something that's been going full speed ahead since the 80s than in a Silicon Valley fad startup.
Even my dad pokes fun at me for using a 30 year old protocol, but not after I pointed out that he still uses HTTP.
I'm more likely to stick around after I get help with whatever issue I came to you for, and contribute back by helping others as I idle in your channel until the end of time.
On Slack, I leave as soon as I'm done getting help because tabs in my browser are precious real estate.
Slack is not a tool built for open source projects to use for communication with their userbase. It's a tool built for teams and it is ill-suited to this use-case.
Gitter is bad for many of the same reasons Slack is. Please don't use it over IRC.
key_insight: FOSS projects should use open, standardized communication tools like IRC that match their values, rather than adopting proprietary platforms that fragment communities and create dependencies on corporate infrastructure.
